primary.question: What are the conditions for a student to proceed to the final year if they have passed fewer than 120 credits in Year One?
primary.expected_answer: A student may be allowed to proceed to the Final Year, following an attempt at reassessment, having passed a minimum of 105 credits, provided they have passed all the core modules for the Year.
primary.actual_answer:
If a student has failed up to 30 credits in Year One, they must pass 120 credits in Year Two. If they have failed between 15 and 30 non-core credits in Year One:

- They can condone (carry forward) failure in up to 15 non-core credits in Year Two if they reassess.
- However, if a total of 30 non-core credits are failed across the degree, no further failures will be condoned.

If fewer than 30 credits were failed, and there is no further failure beyond these limits:

- Up to 15 (or 30 if none failed previously) non-core credits can still be condoned in Year Two after reassessment.

primary.question: What minimum marks are required for passing a taught core module at University of Essex under these rules?
primary.expected_answer: To pass a taught core module, students must achieve a mark of at least 50 and also score at least 40 in both the coursework aggregate mark and exam mark. Marks between 39.5 and 39.9 are rounded up to 40.
primary.actual_answer:
The pass mark for all modules, including core modules, is generally set at 50 for full-time postgraduate certificates and diplomas (as per the documents for 2025-26 academic year). For undergraduate programs, the pass mark is typically 40.

Specifically:
- **Postgraduate Certificates**: The pass mark is 50. [source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/other-pgt-awards/pg-cert-25.pdf]
- **Postgraduate Diplomas**: The pass mark is 50. [source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/other-pgt-awards/pg-dip-25.pdf]
- **Undergraduate Programs (e.g., Three-Year Degree)**: The pass mark is 40. [source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/principle-and-framework/ug-principles-and-framework.pdf]

Core modules must be passed without condonement (allowing a failed grade to be accepted). Therefore, for core taught modules:
- For postgraduate programs: 50.
- For undergraduate programs: 40.

For full-time graduate diplomas in the 2025-26 academic year, the pass mark is also set at 40. [source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/graduate-qualifications/ug-grad-dip-year-1.pdf]

primary.question: What are the general principles for reassessment according to the rules of assessment document?
primary.expected_answer: The general principles for reassessment apply throughout all years, including Year One, Two, Three, and the Final Year, but specific details vary by year.
primary.actual_answer:
The general principles for reassessment according to the rules of assessment documents include:

- If you fail any modules but achieve marks of 20 or above in all modules undertaken, you must take reassessment during the next Term or Summer Break before progressing.
- You can have up to two further attempts at any module; however, your Module Mark(s) for these further attempts will be capped at 40 unless substantiated Extenuating Circumstances apply.
- The Board of Examiners offers all available reassessment opportunities and uses the previous higher mark if a later attempt scores lower.
